meta.scope: Teacher-driven source intake for the software-engineering apprentice. Each record tracks a candidate source from discovery through curation to promotion into benchmark curriculum.
meta.honesty_note: This intake log records real teacher decisions. Sources marked manual_curation_only require original RF-authored episodes — no bulk ingestion. Status fields reflect actual progress, not aspirations.
meta.process: discover -> curate -> promote. Any teacher can follow this process. See docs/teacher-source-curriculum-workflow.md for the full workflow.

intake_records.discovery_notes: Playwright best-practices docs provide clear, public, well-licensed guidance on UI regression testing patterns. Directly relevant to Role Foundry's frontend apprentice because the apprentice ships browser-visible product slices.
intake_records.curation_notes: Selected two best-practice themes grounded in existing RF UI surfaces: user-visible locator discipline and test isolation. Episodes are RF-authored, not copied from Playwright docs.
intake_records.promotion_notes: Promoted as family rf.frontend-apprentice.public.playwright-regression in public-benchmark-pack-v1. Two episodes tied to existing RF screens (scenarios.html, run.html). Rubric authored fresh for RF context.

provenance.rf_authorship: Episodes and rubric are original RF-authored work informed by Playwright best practices. No Playwright prose is copied verbatim.

intake_records.source_name: Google Engineering Practices — code review guides
intake_records.discovery_notes: Google's code review guides are public, well-licensed (CC BY 3.0), and directly relevant to the apprentice's need for diff-critique and review-comment discipline.
intake_records.curation_notes: Curation direction was to turn this into an RF-authored code-review-discipline family grounded in RF diffs. The eventual promotion kept that shape: original RF-authored episodes and rubric, no copied Google prose.
intake_records.promotion_notes: Promoted as family rf.frontend-apprentice.public.code-review-discipline in public-benchmark-pack-v1. Two episodes grounded in existing RF surfaces (scenarios.html, run.html). Rubric authored fresh for RF context — no Google prose copied verbatim.

provenance.rf_authorship: Episodes and rubric are original RF-authored work informed by Google's public code review guidelines. No Google prose is copied verbatim.

intake_records.license: MIT (code/docs only; GitHub issue/PR text has ambiguous downstream rights)
intake_records.discovery_notes: Alpine.js is the apprentice's frontend framework. Stack-adjacent bugfix exemplars could be valuable, but raw GitHub issue/PR/review text should not be assumed to carry clean downstream training rights.
intake_records.curation_notes: Narrowed the Alpine lane to public docs/examples only: x-show, x-cloak, x-transition, and x-bind patterns that match Role Foundry's own Alpine surfaces. Raw GitHub issue/PR/review text remains excluded because downstream rights are ambiguous.
intake_records.promotion_notes: Promoted as family rf.frontend-apprentice.public.alpine-state-patterns in public-benchmark-pack-v1. Two episodes tie Alpine docs/examples to existing RF screens (scenarios.html, run.html). Rubric authored fresh for RF context. Promotion uses docs/examples only, not GitHub discussion text.

provenance.source_type: public documentation + repo examples (manually rewritten into RF-authored episodes)
provenance.rf_authorship: Episodes and rubric are original RF-authored work grounded in Alpine.js docs/examples only. No Alpine GitHub issue/PR/review text is copied or used.

intake_records.license: MIT (harness/packaging); task instances inherit mixed upstream repo licenses
intake_records.discovery_notes: SWE-bench provides real issue-to-patch benchmark structure and evaluation harness ideas. Useful as a reference for benchmark design and as a potential source for teacher-only holdout candidates. NOT suitable for public curriculum: wrong size and shape (broad, backend-heavy, infra-expensive), contamination-prone, and not aligned with the current frontend/product loop.
intake_records.curation_notes: Directive: use SWE-bench material ONLY as a small manually curated teacher-only holdout direction. Do NOT bulk-import. Do NOT treat as first-wave public curriculum. Any SWE-bench-derived holdout candidates must be (1) manually selected by a teacher, (2) rewritten into original RF-authored episodes, (3) stored outside the public benchmark pack, and (4) clearly labeled teacher-only / manual-curation-only with explicit provenance.

teacher_only_holdout_direction.scope: teacher-only holdout candidates only — never public curriculum
teacher_only_holdout_direction.selection_rule: A teacher manually selects a small number (≤10) of SWE-bench task instances that test failure modes relevant to the current apprentice role. Each selected instance is rewritten as an original RF-authored holdout episode.
teacher_only_holdout_direction.storage_rule: Holdout episodes derived from SWE-bench must be stored outside the public benchmark pack, in the local private holdout manifest or a separate teacher-only pack.
teacher_only_holdout_direction.student_visibility: none — SWE-bench-derived holdout candidates must never appear in student-visible curriculum, public benchmark packs, or demo data
teacher_only_holdout_direction.licensing_note: SWE-bench harness is MIT but individual task instances reference upstream repos with varied licenses. Any RF-authored holdout must stand as original work, not a copy of upstream code or discussion.
teacher_only_holdout_direction.blocked_reason: Bulk import is blocked. This record exists to document the teacher-only holdout direction and keep the seam visible for future manual curation.

provenance.source_license: MIT (harness); upstream task repos have mixed licenses
provenance.source_type: benchmark dataset — manual curation only, teacher-only holdout direction
provenance.rf_authorship: No episodes authored yet. Any future holdout episodes must be original RF-authored work, not copies of SWE-bench task descriptions or upstream patches.

status_definitions.discovered: Source identified as potentially relevant. No episodes authored yet.
status_definitions.curated: Source reviewed by a teacher, suitability confirmed, episode authoring planned or in progress.
status_definitions.promoted: At least one RF-authored episode family has been promoted into the benchmark pack from this source.
status_definitions.rejected: Source reviewed and determined unsuitable for this role's curriculum.
